metadata.description: Maps each deduplicated threat to its discovering Phase B specialists for confidence analysis

TECH-H001.title: Broken onlyOwner/onlyManager Modifiers Enable Complete Protocol Takeover
TECH-H001.duplicate_group_description: Broken onlyOwner/onlyManager modifiers in Security.sol evaluate boolean expressions as no-op statements without require/revert, making all protected functions publicly callable and enabling complete protocol takeover

ECON-H004.title: Fee Manipulation via Broken Access Control Enables Sell Lockout and Fund Trapping
ECON-H004.duplicate_group_description: Fee manipulation to ≥100% via broken access control causes arithmetic underflow in sell calculations, permanently blocking all sell transactions and trapping user funds

ECON-H014.title: Unbacked ERC20 Minting via Broken Access Control on Factory Setter
ECON-H014.duplicate_group_description: Broken access control allows anyone to replace the ERC20 factory via setERC20Factory, enabling deployment of malicious ERC20 tokens that break the 1:1 backing invariant

ECON-H002.title: Unprotected setCurves() Enables Complete FeeSplitter Drain via Malicious Contract
ECON-H002.duplicate_group_description: FeeSplitter.setCurves() has no access control modifier, allowing anyone to point it to a malicious contract returning fabricated balances/supply, draining all ETH via claimFees

ECON-H003.title: FeeSplitter Insolvency via Transfer-Based Fee Double-Claiming
ECON-H003.duplicate_group_description: Internal token transfers (_transfer via transferCurvesToken/transferAllCurvesTokens) do not call feeRedistributor.onBalanceChange, leaving recipient's userFeeOffset at 0 and enabling retroactive claims of all historical fees

TECH-H004.title: Weaponized onBalanceChange Wipes Victim's Unclaimed Fees Due to Broken Access Control
TECH-H004.duplicate_group_description: FeeSplitter.onBalanceChange overwrites userFeeOffset without calling updateFeeCredit first, permanently destroying accumulated unclaimed fees — occurs passively on every buy/sell AND can be weaponized via broken onlyManager

OPS-H007.title: Protocol Fee and Referral Fee Permanently Locked in Curves Contract on Sells
OPS-H007.duplicate_group_description: Protocol fee and referral fee (when undefined) on sell transactions are deducted from seller payout but never forwarded to destinations, permanently locking ETH in Curves contract with no recovery mechanism

TECH-H009.title: Referral Fee on Sells Permanently Locked When No Referral Defined
TECH-H009.duplicate_group_description: Referral fee on sell transactions is deducted from seller's payout but the .call is skipped when referralFeeDestination is address(0), permanently locking the ETH

TECH-H007.title: DoS on All Trading by Wrapping All Tokens to ERC20 — addFees Reverts on Zero totalSupply
TECH-H007.duplicate_group_description: All circulating tokens wrapped to ERC20 via withdraw() causes FeeSplitter.totalSupply to return 0, making addFees revert with NoTokenHolders and halting all buy/sell operations

TECH-H008.title: Withdraw-Deposit Cycle Exploits Stale Fee Offsets for Disproportionate Fee Claims
TECH-H008.duplicate_group_description: Withdraw/deposit cycle exploits stale fee offsets — withdraw reduces internal balance without updating FeeSplitter, fees accumulate at higher per-token rate, deposit restores balance, user claims inflated fees retroactively

TECH-H010.title: Excess ETH from Buy Overpayment Permanently Locked in Curves Contract
TECH-H010.duplicate_group_description: Excess ETH from buy overpayment (msg.value > price + totalFee) is permanently locked in Curves contract with no refund mechanism

TECH-H011.title: Any Token Holder Can Deploy ERC20 via withdraw() Bypassing Subject's onlyTokenSubject
TECH-H011.duplicate_group_description: Any token holder can trigger ERC20 deployment via withdraw() bypassing onlyTokenSubject modifier on mint(), forcing deployment with default metadata and permanently locking the subject out of custom branding

TECH-H019.title: Bonding curve getPrice underflows for supply=0 and amount>1, preventing multi-mint bootstrap and causing unintended DoS for initial buy-with-name/presale setup
TECH-H019.duplicate_group_description: getPrice arithmetic underflow when supply=0 and amount>1 due to (supply-1+amount) evaluating supply-1 first in Solidity 0.8.7 checked arithmetic, preventing multi-token initial purchase

TECH-H012.title: FeeSplitter userTokens Array Accumulates Unbounded Duplicates Causing DoS on Batch Claims
TECH-H012.duplicate_group_description: FeeSplitter.onBalanceChange pushes tokens to userTokens array without duplicate check on every call, causing unbounded array growth that DoS's getUserTokensAndClaimable and batchClaiming

TECH-H020.title: buy amount=0 allows zero-cost state updates and downstream bloat (ownedCurvesTokenSubjects), enabling gas griefing and storage growth
TECH-H020.duplicate_group_description: Buying 0 tokens is not prevented — getPrice returns 0 for amount=0, allowing zero-cost transactions that trigger state updates and array bloat in ownedCurvesTokenSubjects and FeeSplitter.userTokens

TECH-H002.title: FeeSplitter.setCurves() Completely Unprotected — Enables Fee Drain via Fabricated Balances

TECH-H003.title: Fee Theft via Token Transfer — Recipient Claims All Historical Fees

TECH-H005.title: onBalanceChange Systematically Destroys Buyer's Accumulated Fees on Every Purchase

TECH-H006.title: Protocol Fee on Sells Permanently Locked in Curves Contract — Never Forwarded

TECH-H013.title: Public takeover of Curves fee routing enables immediate ETH drainage via 100% buy-fee or malicious FeeSplitter

TECH-H014.title: FeeSplitter curves pointer can be hijacked to fabricate balances and drain all ETH in FeeSplitter

TECH-H015.title: Anyone can wipe or distort user holder-fee entitlements by calling FeeSplitter.onBalanceChange directly

TECH-H016.title: Internal token transfers enable retroactive fee claims (offset not updated), draining FeeSplitter ETH

TECH-H017.title: sell value accounting bug permanently locks protocolFee/referralFee in Curves during sells, creating trapped ETH with no withdrawal path

TECH-H018.title: FeeSplitter payout via transfer() can permanently block claims for contract wallets, stranding earned ETH
